segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: So Jane – you'll be off to Denmark soon to do your work placement.
segments.zh: 简，你很快就要去丹麦实习了吧。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Yes, I'm really looking forward to it and I've just started packing up all my books to put in storage.
segments.zh: 是的，我真的很期待，而且我刚把所有书打包好准备寄存。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Well, I hope they don't get spoilt.
segments.zh: 嗯，希望它们别受潮损坏。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: It's OK – my grandfather works in a bookshop and he told me how to pack them.
segments.zh: 没事——我爷爷在书店工作，他教我怎么打包。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Oh, that's helpful.
segments.zh: 哦，那挺有用的。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He says you have to support the spine otherwise the paper can come away from the cover.
segments.zh: 他说必须支撑住书脊，否则纸张会从封面上脱落。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Yeah – that's obvious.
segments.zh: 是啊——那很明显。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He also told me to pack them flat in the box not on their side – again because they can bend and if you leave them like that for, say, a year, it's quite hard to get them back to their normal shape.
segments.zh: 他还告诉我把书平放在箱子里，不要侧放——同样是因为书会弯曲，如果那样放上一年，很难恢复原状。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Well, it's pretty clear that ruins them, but a lot of people just can't be bothered to protect their books.
segments.zh: 嗯，很明显是那个毁了书，但很多人就是懒得去保护自己的书。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He always says it's such a shame that publishers don't use better-quality paper.
segments.zh: 他总是说出版商不用质量更好的纸真是太可惜了。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: It's the acid in the paper that causes the problem, isn't it?
segments.zh: 是纸里的酸导致的问题，对吧？

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Yeah – that's why old books go yellow.
segments.zh: 对——那就是旧书变黄的原因。你知道我祖父给我的有些书已经那样了。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: You know some of the books my grandfather's given me are like that already.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Oh . . .

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: I should dump them really if they're going to deteriorate further, but I'd feel bad.
segments.zh: 如果它们还会继续损坏的话，我真该把它们扔掉，但我会觉得不好受。它们总会让我想起他。你知道，他可是个收藏家。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: They'll always remind me of him.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He's quite a collector, you know.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Well, if they're important to you . . .
segments.zh: 嗯，如果它们对你很重要的话……

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Yeah – I'd regret just throwing them away.
segments.zh: 对——我会后悔直接把它们扔掉的。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: You know, maybe it's because I was taught to treasure books . . . but I hate seeing students force open the pages – of paperbacks.
segments.zh: 你知道吗，可能是因为我从小就被教育要爱惜书……但我真看不惯学生硬掰平装书的书页。他们用力太大，结果把书脊都弄断了。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: They press so hard they end up breaking the spine.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: I know, but unfortunately, paperbacks aren't designed to last a long time and people know that.
segments.zh: 我懂，但可惜平装书本来就不耐用，大家也知道这点。精装书就没那么脆弱。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Hardbacks aren't quite as weak.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Yeah, they're different, I suppose.
segments.zh: 是啊，我觉得它们确实不一样。但我还是觉得现在的人不像以前那样珍惜精装书了。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: But I still don't think people value hardbacks like they used to.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Well, they aren't decorative, are they, like other objects.
segments.zh: 嗯，它们不像其他物品那样有装饰性。而且现在人们也不像以前那样把它们摆在书架上了。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Plus, nowadays, people don't keep them out on shelves as much as they used to.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: That's such a pity.
segments.zh: 真可惜。我去别人家时，如果桌子上有本色彩鲜艳的书，那总是最先吸引我的东西。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: When I visit someone – if they have, say, a colourful book on a table, it's the first thing I'm drawn to.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: I agree – and book covers can be a work of art in themselves.
segments.zh: 我同意——书的封面本身就是艺术品。有些封面真的很抢眼。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Some are really eye-catching.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: I've always been taught to handle books carefully.
segments.zh: 我一直被教导要小心拿书。如果你看别人从书架上取书，他们通常动作都不对。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: If you watch someone take a book off a shelf, well, they usually do it wrong.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Ah, my grandfather says, you should put your hand right over the top of the book . . . or if you can't do that, pull the other books on the shelf aside so that you can hold the whole cover.
segments.zh: 啊，我爷爷说，你应该把手放在书的顶部……如果做不到，就把旁边的书拨开，这样就能握住整个封面了。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: When did you learn all this?
segments.zh: 你什么时候知道这些的？

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He watched me pull a heavy book off the shelf when I was small, and it fell on the floor and broke apart.
segments.zh: 我小时候，他看着我拿一本很重的书从书架上掉下来，摔在地上裂开了。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Oh dear!
segments.zh: 哎呀！

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: I can still remember it!
segments.zh: 我现在还记得！

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: You know what I really like?
segments.zh: 你知道我真正喜欢什么吗？

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: What?
segments.zh: 什么？

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: The smell of new books.
segments.zh: 新书的味道。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Me too.
segments.zh: 我也是。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: My parents used to laugh at me when I was a kid because I loved putting books up to my nose.
segments.zh: 我小时候特别喜欢把书凑到鼻子跟前闻，我爸妈老笑话我，简直跟读书一样上瘾！

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Almost as much as reading them!

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: New books aren't cheap, though, are they?
segments.zh: 不过新书可不便宜，对吧？

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: I guess we're lucky we can buy them.
segments.zh: 我觉得我们能买得起书已经很幸运了。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: My grandfather stocks second-hand books as well as new ones and they don't smell quite as good.
segments.zh: 我爷爷既卖新书也卖二手书，但二手书的味道就没那么好了。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。但他想让别人知道他有这些书，所以就把它们摆在店里，但得用梯子才能够到。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: But he likes people to know that he has them.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: So, he puts them out in the shop but makes sure you need a ladder to get them.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错，这样就能防止偷书了！

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: That would prevent any thefts!

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Uhuh.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Does he stock books for children?
segments.zh: 他卖儿童书吗？

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He does.
segments.zh: 他确实如此。他特别喜欢鼓励孩子们阅读；他总是说，他小时候常常抱着一堆书坐在楼梯下面，把它们全部读完。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He particularly likes to encourage kids to read; he always says that he used to sit under the stairs as a child with a pile of books and read them all.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Is that where he keeps them, then?
segments.zh: 那么，他就是把书放在那里吗？

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Not exactly – he's got a dedicated area on the ground floor with cushions so that parents can enter with their toddlers, go there and spend some time reading to them.
segments.zh: 不完全是——他在一楼专门设了一个区域，放有坐垫，这样家长可以带着幼儿进去，在那里花时间给他们读书。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Oh cool.
segments.zh: 哦，真酷。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: And then there's a place for pushchairs by the front door.
segments.zh: 前门旁边还有一个放婴儿车的地方。还有一个咖啡馆，如果有人需要茶点的话。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: And a café if anyone needs refreshments.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: That's good to know.
segments.zh: 知道了真好。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: As I said, it's a big shop and there's a storage area out the back as well.
segments.zh: 就像我说的，这家店很大，后面还有一个储藏区。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Oh, what does he keep there?
segments.zh: 哦，他把什么放在那里？他想扔掉的书吗？

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Books he wants to throw away?

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He hardly ever throws anything away – he just leaves unwanted books by the front door for customers to take.
segments.zh: 他几乎从不扔东西——只是把不要的书放在前门，让顾客拿走。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Well, that's very nice.
segments.zh: 嗯，那挺好的。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Yeah – and books people or institutions have requested, they all go at the far end.
segments.zh: 是啊——还有别人或机构预订的书，都放在最里头。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Oh.
segments.zh: 哦。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He thinks it's best to keep these out of the main shopping area as they're boxed and new.
segments.zh: 他觉得最好把这些书放在主要购物区外面，因为它们是盒装的新书。

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Did you get your coursebooks from him?
segments.zh: 你的课本是从他那里买的吗？

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: Naturally.
segments.zh: 当然。他有很多大学的教材。以前他把这些书放在一楼，但现在我家乡有了一所新大学，他就把书搬到楼下吸引学生。这些书其实就在咖啡店里，放在周围矮书架上。

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He stocks books for a lot of the colleges.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: He used to keep these books on the first floor, but now there's a new university in my hometown, he's moved them downstairs to attract the students.

segments.speaker: JANE
segments.en: They're actually part of the coffee shop, on low shelves all around it.

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: Pretty central then.
segments.zh: 那位置挺中心的。你改天得带我去看看！

segments.speaker: KIERAN
segments.en: You'll have to take me there some time!

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Tree planting now dominates political and popular agendas and is often presented as an easy answer to the climate crisis, as well as a way for business corporations to offset their carbon emissions.
segments.zh: 如今植树造林占据了政治和公众议程的主导地位，常被当作气候危机的简单解决方案，也是企业抵消碳排放的一种方式。但不幸的是，植树并不像一些人想的那么简单。如果在错误的地方种错了树，可能弊远大于利，既无助于人类，也无益于环境。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But unfortunately, tree planting isn't as straightforward as some people think.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When the wrong trees are planted in the wrong place, it can do considerably more damage than good, failing to help either people or the environment.

items.prompt: Kieran thinks the packing advice given by Jane's grandfather is

pairs.note: 题干中未直接出现，但原文中该短语是建议的核心内容，与选项A对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Kieran认为Jane的祖父的打包建议是什么，定位句直接给出建议内容：支撑书脊，否则纸张会脱离封面，对应选项A。

items.prompt: How does Jane feel about the books her grandfather has given her?

pairs.note: 题干问感觉，原文直接说feel bad，是原词复现。
题干说爷爷给的书，原文说这些书让她想起他，是同一意思的不同表达。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'feel bad'和'remind me of him'，直接对应题干中的'feel about the books'和'her grandfather'，因此答案C（舍不得扔）正确。

items.prompt: Jane and Kieran agree that hardback books should be

pairs.note: hardback books 被替换为 a colourful book，指代精装书

pairs.p: it's the first thing I'm drawn to
pairs.note: 题干中的赞同观点对应原文中‘首先被吸引’的表述

paraphrase.explain: 题干问两人同意精装书应该怎样，定位句提到‘a colourful book’是‘the first thing I'm drawn to’，对应选项A‘be displayed prominently’（被显眼展示）。

items.prompt: While talking about taking a book from a shelf, Jane

pairs.q: taking a book from a shelf
pairs.p: pull a heavy book off the shelf
pairs.note: 题干'从书架上拿书'对应原文'把一本厚书从书架上拉下来'，动作和场景一致。

paraphrase.explain: 题干描述'从书架上拿书'，定位句中有'pull a heavy book off the shelf'，动作相同，可定位到该句。

items.prompt: What do Jane and Kieran suggest about new books?

pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位。
题干问建议，原文用反问句表达观点，属于同义转述。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'new books'直接定位到原文，再根据反问句'aren't cheap, though, are they?'理解说话人的暗示。

items.prompt: Where does Jane's grandfather keep each of the following types of books in his shop?

pairs.note: keep 被替换为 puts them out，表示放置
几乎相同，只是 his 变为 the

paraphrase.explain: 题干问祖父把书放在店里的哪里，定位句提到他把书放在店里但需要梯子才能拿到，对应选项 D（需要梯子的地方）。

items.prompt: Where does Jane's grandfather keep each of the following types of books in his shop?

pairs.note: 'keep'被替换为'got a dedicated area'，表示存放书籍的特定区域。
'in his shop'具体化为'ground floor'，是商店内的位置。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'books'和'keep'定位到原文中'got a dedicated area'和'books'，再根据'ground floor'确定位置。

items.prompt: Where does Jane's grandfather keep each of the following types of books in his shop?

pairs.note: keep 和 leaves 都表示'放置'，但 leaves 更强调'留下'。
in his shop 是笼统地点，by the front door 是具体位置，属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问书放在哪里，定位句提到'by the front door'，对应'keep'的'leaves'，直接定位到答案。

items.prompt: Where does Jane's grandfather keep each of the following types of books in his shop?

pairs.q: books people or institutions have requested
pairs.p: books people or institutions have requested
pairs.note: 题干中的'requested'与原文完全一致，直接定位。
'keep'（存放）与'go'（被放置）是同义替换，表示书籍被放在某处。
题干中的'at the far end'与原文完全一致，直接定位位置。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问每种书放在哪里，定位句提到'requested'的书'go at the far end'，因此答案对应'at the far end'。

items.prompt: Where does Jane's grandfather keep each of the following types of books in his shop?

pairs.note: 题干中的keep与原文中的keep完全一致，直接对应。
题干中的books与原文中的books完全一致，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是Jane的祖父在书店里把每种类型的书放在哪里，定位句直接提到了他存放书的位置变化，因此通过关键词keep、books、in his shop即可定位到原文。
